title: Java Primitive Streams
description: Practice IntStream, mapToInt, summaryStatistics, and boxed conversion.
learningGoals: Convert object streams to primitive streams with mapToInt.
Use IntStream summaryStatistics and boxed when object results are needed again.

choices: Compilation fails because mapToInt returns Stream<Integer>
completionChecks.explanation: mapToInt(Integer::parseInt) creates an IntStream of 4, 8, and 15. summaryStatistics then reports the maximum primitive int as 15.

completionChecks.prompt: Why is boxed() used on the IntStream range?
choices: To turn IntStream elements into Integer objects before collecting into a List
To force the stream to run in parallel
To parse strings as primitive ints
completionChecks.explanation: boxed() adapts an IntStream back to Stream<Integer>, which is useful when a collection of wrapper objects is the desired result.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the final println output?
choices: Compilation fails because getMax returns OptionalInt
completionChecks.explanation: The maximum parsed value is 15, and boxed contains 1, 2, and 3, so boxed.size() is 3. The printed sum is 18.
